title: IPLR Transfer Callback v1
description: Wire format for the webhook Lambda POSTs to Drupal at /api/iplr/webhook/media-transfer once a transfer reaches a terminal state. Body is signed with HMAC-SHA256 (hex-encoded) using the secret resolved from callback_secret_ref in the trigger; signature lives in X-Webhook-Signature. Drupal updates the corresponding ProductLoadItem on receipt.

item_id.description: Echoed from the trigger. Drupal loads ProductLoadItem by this id.

request_id.description: Echoed from the trigger. Drupal verifies the item belongs to this request before updating.

operation.description: Echoed from the trigger so Drupal updates the right field set (media_* vs image_*).

status.description: Terminal state only. Lambda must not POST 'pending' or 'in_progress' callbacks; intermediate states are tracked Drupal-side via the cron janitor.

error_code.description: Required when status=error. Machine-readable failure mode. Drupal surfaces a user-facing message keyed off this; 'internal' is the catch-all and should be rare.

error_message.description: Free-text detail. Logged to watchdog and stored on the item's media_metadata.transfer_error. Not user-facing.

bytes_transferred.description: Required on status=complete. Stored on the item's media_metadata for audit.

s3_etag.description: Required on status=complete. The dest object's ETag from the multipart-upload completion. Stored for audit.

examples.error_message: Drive API returned 401 invalid_grant; access token had expired before transfer started
